zh.lifeSummary: 當Bridget Copley於1535年出生在英國素瑞加頓時，她的父親Sir Roger Copley62歲，母親Elizabeth Shelly22歲。她於大約1525年與Robert Leeche of Norwich在英國結婚。她去世於1565年之後在英國諾福爾可武德利辛。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Bridget Copley于1535年出生在英国素瑞加頓时，她的父亲Sir Roger Copley62岁，母亲Elizabeth Shelly22岁。她于大约1525年与Robert Leeche of Norwich在英国结婚。她去世于1565年之后在英国諾福爾可武德利辛。

nameMeanings.name: Copley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Yorkshire): habitational name from any of various places called Copley, for example in County Durham, Staffordshire, and Yorkshire, from the Old English personal name Coppa (apparently a byname for a tall man) or from copp ‘hilltop’ + lēah ‘woodland clearing’.

nameMeanings.name: Bridget
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of Gaelic Brighid (pronounced ‘breed’); . This was the name of an ancient Celtic goddess, which in Gaulish would have been Brigindos, meaning ‘the exalted one’. St Brigid of Kildare ( c.450– c.525 ) is one of the patron saints of Ireland. Very few facts are known about her life. She founded a religious house for women at Kildare, and is said to be have been buried at Downpatrick, where St Patrick and St Columba were also buried. Many of the stories of miracles told about St Brigid seem to be Christianized versions of pagan legends concerning the goddess. The popularity of the name was further reinforced throughout Europe, especially in Scandinavia in the form Birgit , as the name of the patron saint of Sweden ( 1304–73 ). She was a noblewoman of Irish stock who, after the death of her husband, founded an order of nuns, the Bridgettines. Later she went to Rome and attempted to introduce religious reforms there.

variants.name: Bridgid
meanings: Anglicized form of Gaelic Brighid (pronounced ‘breed’); . This was the name of an ancient Celtic goddess, which in Gaulish would have been Brigindos, meaning ‘the exalted one’. variants.name: Brigid
meanings: Traditional name meaning ‘the exalted one’. See Bridget .

variants.name: Brigit
meanings: Traditional name meaning ‘the exalted one’. See Bridget .

variants.name: Brighid
meanings: Traditional name meaning ‘the exalted one’. See Bridget .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Bridget Copley was born in 1535, in Gatton, Surrey, England, her father, Sir Roger Copley, was 62 and her mother, Elizabeth Shelly, was 22. She married Robert Leeche of Norwich about 1525, in England. She died after 1565, in Woodrising, Norfolk, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Bridget Copley(이)가 1535년 영국 수리 가턴에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Roger Copley(은)는 62세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Shelly(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 대략 1525년 영국에서 Robert Leeche of Norwich(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1565년 이후 영국 노르폴크 우드리징에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Bridget Copley родилась в 1535 г. в местности гаттон, сурри, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir Roger Copley было 62 года, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Shelly, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Robert Leeche of Norwich около 1525 г. в местности Англия. Она умерла после 1565 г. в местности вудрисинг, норфолк, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Bridget Copley nació en 1535, en Gatton, Surrey, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Roger Copley, tenía 62 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Shelly, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Robert Leeche of Norwich aproximadamente 1525, en Inglaterra. Murió después de 1565, en Woodrising, Norfolk, Inglaterra.
